rulings.Case Title: MARK BIRKHEAD VS. OLIVER PARKER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To Defts First Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, April 3, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANTs OLIVER PARKER and MICROSOFT CORPORATION's Motion To Compel Further Response To Defendants First Request For Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. Plaintiff failed to Oppose the motion to compel and the facts reveal that Plaintiff failed to respond separately to each document request pursuant to CCP 2031.210(a). Plaintiff is ordered to Amend his responses to provide separate responses to each of the 49 document requests within 30 days of the hearing and to produce the documents at that time as well. The response must either affirm he will comply with the request or affirm a diligent search and reasonably inquiry was made and specify whether the inability to comply is because a document never existed, was lost or misplaced, or has never been or is no longer in plaintiff's legal possession, custody or control. Sanctions in the amount of $2,500 is granted. Moving party shall provide a Proposed Order tracking the Tentative Ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE, VS. RAYMOND TANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton To Strike The Answer Of Deft For Misuse Of Discovery And Failure To Comply Wiht Court Order To Respond To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, April 3, 2014, Line 2,PLAINTIFF FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE's Motion To Strike The Answer Of Defendant For Misuse Of Discovery And Failure To Comply Wiht Court Order To Respond To Discovery Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Defendant should be prepared to explain why verified responses to discovery have not been provided months after agreeing to provide the responses and why the Court should not Strike the Answer after a Court has ordered Defendant to provide responses. Sanctions are granted. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: KATHERINE FRAZER VS. DR. VIC PRIETO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 3, 2014, Line 17, DEFENDANT HOYMAN HONG, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with 30 days leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e) and (f).) As to the first cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege a medical negligence cause of action against defendants. Plaintiff is also given leave to amend to clarify which doctor treated her, for what reason she sought treatment from each doctor, and what treatment was actually received. As to the second cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege, if she can in good faith, facts establishing a fraud cause of action. As to the third cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege, if she can in good faith, facts establishing a slander cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: EDUARDO BATIZ ORANTES VS. ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 3, 2014, Line 20, DEFENDANTS ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY AND STEPHENS INSTITUTE DOING BUSINESS AS ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY'S Motion To Compel Arbitration. Granted. The action is stayed pending the arbitration before the American Arbitration Association. Plaintiff has not met his burden to demonstrate that the arbitration provision is unconscionable. Although there is minimal evidence of procedural unconscionability, Plaintiff has not demonstrated the high degree of substantive unconscionability necessary to render the arbitration provision unenforceable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT KURTZ VS. DIGNITY HEALTH et al*****TRANSFERRED TO KERN COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Transfer; Stip
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 3, 2014, Line 22, DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH DBA MERCY HOSPITALS OF BAKERSFIELD'S Motion To Transfer. Granted. No opposition filed. Per the parties' stipulation, the case is transferred to Kern County and Defendant shall pay all costs for the transfer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
